sections.label: Section 1498
sections.translation: O Pelasgia , land of my birth , and Mycenae , my home !

sections.label: Section 1500
sections.translation: Is it on Perseus ’ citadel you call , that town Cyclopean workmen built ?

sections.label: Section 1502
sections.translation: To be a light to Hellas did you rear me , and so I do not say No to death .

sections.label: Section 1504
sections.original: κλέος γὰρ οὔ σε μὴ λίπῃ .
sections.translation: You are right ; no fear that fame will ever desert you !

sections.label: Section 1505
sections.translation: Hail to you , bright lamp of day and light of Zeus ! A different life , a different lot is henceforth mine . Farewell I bid you , light beloved ! Exit Iphigenia . .

sections.label: Section 1510
sections.translation: Behold the maiden on her way , the destroyer of Ilium ’ s town and the Phrygians , with garlands twined about her head , and drops of lustral water on her , soon to be sprinkled with her gushing blood

sections.label: Section 1515
sections.translation: the altar of a murderous goddess , when her shapely neck is severed . [*]

sections.label: Section 1517
sections.translation: For you fair streams of a father ’ s pouring and lustral waters are in store , for you Achaea ’ s army is waiting , eager

sections.label: Section 1520
sections.translation: to reach the citadel of Ilium . But let us celebrate Artemis , the daughter of Zeus , queen among the gods , as if upon some happy chance .

sections.label: Section 1524
sections.translation: O lady revered , delighting in human

sections.label: Section 1525
sections.translation: sacrifice , send on its way to Phrygia ’ s land the army of the Hellenes , to Troy ’ s abodes of guile , and grant that Agamemnon may wreathe his head with deathless fame ,

sections.label: Section 1530
sections.translation: a crown of fairest glory for the spearmen of Hellas .

sections.label: Section 1532
sections.translation: Come forth , O Clytemnestra , daughter of Tyndareus , from the tent , to hear my news .

sections.label: Section 1534
sections.translation: I heard your voice and have come

sections.label: Section 1535
sections.translation: in sad dismay and fearful dread , not sure but what you have arrived with tidings of some fresh trouble for me besides the present woe .

sections.label: Section 1537b
sections.translation: No , rather I want to unfold to you a strange and marvellous story about your child .

sections.label: Section 1539
sections.original: μὴ μέλλε τοίνυν , ἀλλὰ φράζʼ ὅσον τάχος .
sections.translation: Do not delay , then , but speak at once .

sections.label: Section 1540
sections.original: ἀλλʼ , ὦ φίλη δέσποινα , πᾶν πεύσῃ σαφῶς .
sections.translation: Dear mistress , you shall learn all clearly ; from the outset will I tell it , unless my memory fails me somewhat and confuses my tongue in its account . As soon as we reached the grove of Artemis , the child of Zeus , and the flowery meadows ,

sections.label: Section 1545
sections.translation: where the Achaean troops were gathered , bringing your daughter with us , at once the Argive army began assembling ; but when king Agamemnon saw the maiden on her way to the grove to be sacrificed , he gave one groan , and , turning away his face , let the tears burst

sections.label: Section 1550
sections.translation: from his eyes , as he held his robe before them . But the maid , standing close by her father , spoke thus : O my father , here I am ; willingly I offer my body for my country and all Hellas ,

sections.label: Section 1555
sections.original: θῦσαι δίδωμʼ ἑκοῦσα πρὸς βωμὸν θεᾶς
sections.translation: that you may lead me to the altar of the goddess and sacrifice me , since this is Heaven ’ s ordinance . May good luck be yours for any help that I afford ! and may you obtain the victor ’ s gift and come again to the land of your fathers . So then let none of the Argives lay hands on me ,

sections.label: Section 1560
sections.translation: for I will bravely yield my neck without a word .

sections.label: Section 1561
sections.original: τοσαῦτʼ ἔλεξε · πᾶς δʼ ἐθάμβησεν κλύων
sections.translation: She spoke ; and each man marvelled , as he heard the maiden ’ s brave speech . But in the midst Talthybius stood up , for this was his duty , and bade the army refrain from word or deed ;

sections.label: Section 1565
sections.original: Κάλχας δʼ ὁ μάντις ἐς κανοῦν χρυσήλατον
sections.translation: and Calchas , the seer , drawing a sharp sword from its scabbard laid it in a basket of beaten gold , and crowned the maiden ’ s head . Then the son of Peleus , taking the basket and with it lustral water in his hand , ran round the altar of the goddess

sections.label: Section 1570
sections.original: ἔλεξε δʼ · Ὦ παῖ Ζηνός , ὦ θηροκτόνε ,
sections.translation: uttering these words : O Artemis , you child of Zeus , slayer of wild beasts , that wheel your dazzling light amid the gloom , accept this sacrifice which we , the army of the Achaeans and Agamemnon with us , offer to you , pure blood from a beautiful maiden ’ s neck ;

sections.label: Section 1575
sections.original: καὶ δὸς γενέσθαι πλοῦν νεῶν ἀπήμονα
sections.translation: and grant us safe sailing for our ships and the sack of Troy ’ s towers by our spears .

sections.label: Section 1577
sections.original: ἐς γῆν δʼ Ἀτρεῖδαι πᾶς στρατός τʼ ἔστη βλέπων .
sections.translation: Meanwhile the sons of Atreus and all the army stood looking on the ground .

sections.label: Section 1578
sections.translation: [ But the priest , seizing his knife , offered up a prayer and was closely scanning the maiden ’ s throat to see where he should strike .

sections.label: Section 1580
sections.original: ἐμοὶ δέ τʼ ἄλγος οὐ μικρὸν εἰσῄει φρενί ,
sections.translation: It was no slight sorrow filled my heart , as I stood by with bowed head ; when there was a sudden miracle ! Each one of us distinctly heard the sound of a blow , [*] but none saw the spot where the maiden vanished . The priest cried out , and all the army took up the cry

sections.label: Section 1585
sections.translation: at the sight of a marvel all unlooked for , due to some god ’ s agency , and passing all belief , although it was seen ; for there upon the ground lay a deer of immense size , magnificent to see , gasping out her life , with whose blood the altar of the goddess was thoroughly bedewed .

sections.label: Section 1590
sections.original: κἀν τῷδε Κάλχας πῶς δοκεῖς χαίρων ἔφη ·
sections.translation: Then spoke Calchas thus — his joy you can imagine — You captains of this leagued Achaean army , do you see this victim , which the goddess has set before her altar , a mountain-roaming deer ? This is more welcome to her by far than the maid ,

sections.label: Section 1595
sections.original: ὡς μὴ μιάνῃ βωμὸν εὐγενεῖ φόνῳ .
sections.translation: that she may not defile her altar by shedding noble blood . Gladly she has accepted it , and is granting us a prosperous voyage for [*] our attack on Ilium . Therefore take heart , sailors , each man of you , and away to your ships , for today

sections.label: Section 1600
sections.translation: we must leave the hollow bays of Aulis and cross the Aegean main .

sections.label: Section 1601a
sections.translation: Then , when the sacrifice was wholly burnt to ashes in the blazing flame , he offered such prayers as were fitting , that the army might win return ; but Agamemnon sends me to tell you this ,

sections.label: Section 1605
sections.original: λέγειν θʼ ὁποίας ἐκ θεῶν μοίρας κυρεῖ
sections.translation: and say what heaven-sent luck is his , and how he has secured undying fame throughout the length of Hellas . Now I was there myself and speak as an eyewitness ; without a doubt your child flew away to the gods . A truce then to your sorrowing , and cease to be angry with your husband ;

sections.label: Section 1610
sections.original: ἀπροσδόκητα δὲ βροτοῖς τὰ τῶν θεῶν ,
sections.translation: for the gods ’ ways with man are not what we expect , and those whom they love , they keep safe ; yes , for this day has seen your daughter dead and living .

sections.label: Section 1613
sections.original: ὡς ἥδομαί τοι ταῦτʼ ἀκούσασʼ ἀγγέλου ·
sections.translation: What joy to hear these tidings from the messenger ! He tells you your child is living still , among the gods .

sections.label: Section 1615
sections.original: ὦ παῖ , θεῶν τοῦ κλέμμα γέγονας ;
sections.translation: Which of the gods , my child , has stolen you ? How am I to address you ? How can I be sure [*] that this is not an idle tale told to cheer me , to make me cease my piteous lamentation for you ?

sections.label: Section 1619
sections.translation: See ! king Agamemnon approaches ,

sections.label: Section 1620
sections.original: τούσδʼ αὐτοὺς ἔχων σοι φράζειν μύθους .
sections.translation: to confirm this story for you .

sections.label: Section 1621
sections.original: γύναι , θυγατρὸς ἕνεκʼ ὄλβιοι γενοίμεθʼ ἄν ·
sections.translation: Lady , we may be counted [*] happy , as far as concerns our daughter ; for in truth she has fellowship with gods . But you must take this tender [*] child and start for home , for the army is looking now to sail .

sections.label: Section 1625
sections.original: καὶ χαῖρε · χρόνια γε τἀμά σοι προσφθέγματα
sections.translation: Fare you well ! it is long before I shall greet you on my return from Troy ; may it be well with you !

sections.label: Section 1627
sections.translation: Son of Atreus , start for Phrygia ’ s land with joy and so return , I pray , after taking from Troy her fairest spoils .
